title: Solo-Dinner Thai Roast
description: Savory, cheese-pull solo-dinner Thai dish featuring pico de gallo sauce, roast, and dash-friendly flavors.
instructions: Season the pork shoulder with salt, black pepper, and fish sauce.
Heat coconut oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and sear the pork shoulder on all sides until browned.
Transfer the pork to a baking dish and roast in the preheated oven for 25 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix together pico de gallo sauce, roasted peanuts, brown sugar, and lime juice in a small bowl.
After 25 minutes, remove the pork from the oven and brush with the pico de gallo sauce mixture.
Return the pork to the oven and continue roasting for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C).
Remove the pork from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Slice the pork into thin strips and sprinkle with shredded mozzarella cheese and chopped green chilies.
Return the pork to the oven and continue roasting for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.
Slice the pork into thin strips and serve with additional pico de gallo sauce and roasted peanuts, if desired.
